reasoning:
The series BAMLC0A0CM tracks the US Corporate Index Option-Adjusted Spread. As of November 7, 2025, the spread is at 0.84%, which is very tight (near historic lows of ~0.75-0.80%). However, the spread recently spiked from 0.75% to 0.84% over a few days. 
For the shortest horizon (Nov 16, 7 days), the statistical tool estimates a 35-38% probability of an increase, as short-term momentum from the recent spike is likely to revert slightly toward the recent trendline (est 0.825). I estimate this at 0.40.
For the medium horizon (Dec 9, 1 month), the probability moves closer to 0.50. Spreads are highly asymmetric; because 0.84% is still low, upside risks start to balance out any short-term reversion. I estimate 0.48.
For the longest horizon (Feb 7, 3 months), the uncertainty increases significantly. Since the current spread of 0.84% is near cyclical lows, there is a much greater probability of macro conditions or credit events widening the spread back toward higher historical averages (which are often >1.00%). I estimate this at 0.55.
